data.content:
2026-04-20 investigation update:
- Primary sample: beat `maestro-5711`, session `term-1776684836608-lz67bp`, lease `maestro-c73b`. Knots step history shows the beat advanced from `ready_to_evaluate` to `shipped` at `2026-04-20T11:37:34.726010Z`, before Foolery logged `turn_ended` at `2026-04-20T11:37:38.990Z`.
- Session log evidence: after `turn_ended`, OpenCode kept emitting `service=bus type=file.watcher.updated publishing` every ~30s and no natural `child_close` happened before manual termination.
- This was not a take-loop follow-up or ship-follow-up issue. `promptSource` stayed `initial`, there is no `take_loop_follow_up_sent`, and Knots disables auto ship follow-up (`supportsAutoFollowUp(memoryManagerType) => false` in `src/lib/memory-manager-commands.ts`).
- Strong code-path hypothesis: after `turn_ended`, Foolery only schedules `doCloseInput()` (2s grace in `src/lib/terminal-manager-types.ts`), which calls `config.httpSession?.interruptTurn(child)` and `child.stdin?.end()` (`src/lib/agent-session-runtime-helpers.ts`). For OpenCode HTTP, `interruptTurn()` is documented as killing the server process, but the implementation only clears `pendingTurn` and returns true (`src/lib/opencode-http-session.ts`). That means the harness assumes EOF on stdin will end `opencode serve`, but the server can remain alive and keep logging watcher/bus events indefinitely.
- This appears systemic for OpenCode interactive sessions, not unique to `maestro-5711`: the server log shows other `ready_to_evaluate` OpenCode sessions (`maestro-5697`, `maestro-ea79`) exhibiting the same periodic `file.watcher.updated` chatter until manually terminated.
